OB-001.definition: Actors provide malicious instructions (via prompt) to override, bypass, alter, or subvert the output of a LLM/agent's system instructions, guardrails, or intended behavior. The injected prompt manipulates the model's context to execute attacker-controlled instructions instead of following its original programming, but preserves the intent of the input.

ai_tech.definition: Actors provide malicious instructions (via prompt) to override, bypass, alter, or subvert the output of a LLM/agent's system instructions, guardrails, or intended behavior. The injected prompt manipulates the model's context to execute attacker-controlled instructions instead of following its original programming, but preserves the intent of the input.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0051.000: LLM Prompt Injection (Direct)
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0093: Prompt Infiltration via Public-Facing Application

ai_subtech.definition: Explicit attempts to override, replace, or modify the model's system instructions, operational directives, or behavioral guidelines through direct user input, causing the model to follow attacker-controlled instructions instead of its intended programming (e.g., Ignore previous instructions).
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0051.000: LLM Prompt Injection (Direct)
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0093: Prompt Infiltration via Public-Facing Application

ai_subtech.definition: Disguising, encoding, or concealing malicious instructions within direct user input to evade detection mechanisms, content filters, and safety classifiers while preserving the malicious intent and functionality of the prompt injection. Examples include: character substitution (leetspeak, homoglyphs, unicode), encoding techniques (e.g., base64, ROT13, hexadecimal, binary), language mixing, whitespace manipulation, semantic obfuscation (synonyms, paraphrasing), and format-based hiding.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0051.000: LLM Prompt Injection (Direct)
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0093: Prompt Infiltration via Public-Facing Application

ai_subtech.definition: Exploiting inter-agent communication by injecting malicious instructions into one agent's output, which is then trusted, processed, and executed by another agent in a multi-agent system.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0051.000: LLM Prompt Injection (Direct)
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0067: LLM Trusted Output Component Manipulation

ai_tech.definition: Actors embed malicious instructions in external data sources (such as web pages, documents, emails, databases, API responses) that the LLM or the agent retrieves and processes, causing hidden instructions to be executed without the user's knowledge or awareness. The attack exploits the model's inability to distinguish between trusted system instructions and untrusted external content.(Source: https://genai.owasp.org/llmrisk/llm01-prompt-injection/ )
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0051.001: LLM Prompt Injection (Indirect)
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0093: Prompt Infiltration via Public-Facing Application
NIST AML: NISTAML.015: Indirect Prompt Injection
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0067: LLM Trusted Output Component Manipulation

ai_subtech.definition: Embedding malicious instructions within external data sources (e.g., documents, web pages, emails, databases, API responses) that an LLM retrieves and processes, causing these external instructions to override or modify the model's intended behavior without the user's knowledge or awareness.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0051.001: LLM Prompt Injection (Indirect)
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0093: Prompt Infiltration via Public-Facing Application
NIST AML: NISTAML.015: Indirect Prompt Injection
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0067: LLM Trusted Output Component Manipulation

ai_subtech.definition: Concealing, encoding, or disguising malicious instructions within external data sources to evade content scanning, input validation, and security filters while ensuring the AI model can still interpret and execute the hidden instructions when the content is retrieved.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0051.001: LLM Prompt Injection (Indirect)
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0093: Prompt Infiltration via Public-Facing Application
NIST AML: NISTAML.015: Indirect Prompt Injection
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0067: LLM Trusted Output Component Manipulation

ai_subtech.definition: Exploiting inter-agent communication by injecting malicious instructions into one agent's output, which is then trusted, processed, and executed by another agent in a multi-agent system.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0051.001: LLM Prompt Injection (Indirect)
NIST AML: NISTAML.015: Indirect Prompt Injection
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0067: LLM Trusted Output Component Manipulation

ai_tech.definition: Actors gain access to LLM/agent systems, functions, data, or resources without proper authorization through exploitation of authentication weaknesses, access control bypasses, or privilege boundaries. This includes both gaining initial unauthorized access and accessing restricted capabilities within a legitimately accessed system.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0051: LLM Prompt Injection
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0018: Manipulate AI Model
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0067: LLM Trusted Output Component Manipulation

ai_subtech.definition: Gradually manipulating or embedding concealed/contradictory objectives within prompts, context, or interactions over multiple turns to shift the model/agent's operational goals away from intended objectives toward attacker-defined ones, causing deviation from aligned behavior.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0051: LLM Prompt Injection
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0018: Manipulate AI Model
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0067: LLM Trusted Output Component Manipulation

ai_subtech.definition: Redirecting an agent's operational goals and objectives through compromising trusted external components it relies on to function, including injecting malicious objectives through compromised tools, poisoned external resources, malicious prompt templates, or corrupted dependencies.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0051: LLM Prompt Injection
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0018: Manipulate AI Model
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0067: LLM Trusted Output Component Manipulation
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0010: AI Supply Chain Compromise
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0093: Prompt Infiltration via Public-Facing Application

ai_tech.definition: Actors deliberately insert, alter, or create malicious content across various modalities (such as text, image, audio, video, or sensor streams) to influence, corrupt, or exploit an AI system's fusion, interpretation, or decision-making processes. Attacks exploit the integration points where different data types are combined.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0051: LLM Prompt Injection
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0050: Command and Scripting Interpreter
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0067: LLM Trusted Output Component Manipulation
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0043: Craft Adversarial Data

ai_subtech.definition: Embedding malicious instructions, prompts, or data within images using techniques such as steganography, adversarial stickers, embedded text, or visual triggers. These elements are often imperceptible to humans but are extracted, interpreted, or acted upon by vision language models or downstream agents. The image serves as a carrier for a linguistic or symbolic payload intended to influence model behavior.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0051: LLM Prompt Injection
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0050: Command and Scripting Interpreter
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0067: LLM Trusted Output Component Manipulation
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0043: Craft Adversarial Data

ai_subtech.definition: Modifying visual content across any modality by altering pixels, structure, or image features to influence model perception or decision-making. This includes adding noise, applying transformations, overlaying patterns or text, or generating multiple manipulated variants to exploit weaknesses in vision encoders, robustness layers, or safety filters. The manipulation targets how the model interprets the image itself rather than embedding hidden instructions.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0051: LLM Prompt Injection
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0050: Command and Scripting Interpreter
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0067: LLM Trusted Output Component Manipulation
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0043: Craft Adversarial Data

ai_subtech.definition: Hidden, inaudible, or unintelligible voice commands and instructions embedded in audio streams using ultrasonic frequencies, backmasking, or steganographic techniques that automatic speech recognition (ASR) models interpret as valid instructions.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0043: Craft Adversarial Data
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0015: Evade AI Model

ai_subtech.definition: Embedding harmful content or malicious instructions in video streams through specific frames, QR-like triggers, or temporal injection that misleads multimodal model perception and bypasses guardrails or defensive measures.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0043: Craft Adversarial Data
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0015: Evade AI Model

OB-002.definition: Actors employ adversarial technique that circumvents or defeats a model's safety guardrails, content filters, or usage restrictions, enabling output generation that violates the system's policies or intended constraints. The resulting harm comes from whatever guardrail was circumvented: for example, causing the system to violate its operators' policies, bypass safety guardrails and result in unsafe output. In a multi-agent system, jailbreaks can become more complex. Jailbreak prompts could be ingested by one agent while designed to target another agent downstream or parts of the jailbreak could be generated as part of multiple agent interactions after an initial trigger payload. These multi-agent jailbreak prompts will likely present differently than standard jailbreak attacks that are a single prompt or multi-turn interaction with a single chatbot.

ai_tech.definition: Actors employ adversarial technique that circumvents or defeats a model's safety guardrails, content filters, or usage restrictions, enabling output generation that violates the system's policies or intended constraints. The resulting harm comes from whatever guardrail was circumvented: for example, causing the system to violate its operators' policies, bypass safety guardrails and result in unsafe output. In a multi-agent system, jailbreaks can become more complex. Jailbreak prompts could be ingested by one agent while designed to target another agent downstream or parts of the jailbreak could be generated as part of multiple agent interactions after an initial trigger payload. These multi-agent jailbreak prompts will likely present differently than standard jailbreak attacks that are a single prompt or multi-turn interaction with a single chatbot.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0093: Prompt Infiltration via Public-Facing Application
NIST AML: NISTAML.015: Indirect Prompt Injection

ai_subtech.definition: Constructing elaborate scenarios, fictional contexts, or alternative frameworks that reframe harmful requests as acceptable within the created context, causing the model to operate under the attacker's contextual assumptions rather than its safety guidelines (e.g., "Do anything now" jailbreak)
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0093: Prompt Infiltration via Public-Facing Application
NIST AML: NISTAML.015: Indirect Prompt Injection

ai_subtech.definition: Disguising jailbreak attempts through encoding, linguistic tricks, character substitution, or creative formatting to evade jailbreak detection systems while preserving the intent to bypass safety measures.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0093: Prompt Infiltration via Public-Facing Application
NIST AML: NISTAML.015: Indirect Prompt Injection

ai_subtech.definition: Using carefully crafted semantic techniques, logical frameworks, or philosophical arguments to convince the model that providing harmful information aligns with its goals, values, or operational principles, effectively arguing the model into jailbreak compliance.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0093: Prompt Infiltration via Public-Facing Application
NIST AML: NISTAML.015: Indirect Prompt Injection

ai_subtech.definition: Exploiting specific tokens, special characters, control sequences, or tokenization behaviors to manipulate model processing, bypass safety filters, or trigger unintended model behaviors that enable jailbreak success.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0043: Craft Adversarial Data
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0093: Prompt Infiltration via Public-Facing Application
NIST AML: NISTAML.015: Indirect Prompt Injection

ai_subtech.definition: Coordinating multiple AI agents to collectively bypass safety measures, where individual agents perform seemingly benign tasks that combine to achieve jailbreak, or where compromised agents assist others in circumventing restrictions.
mappings: NIST AML: NISTAML.015: Indirect Prompt Injection

OB-003.definition: Actors introduce a malicious agent that impersonates a legitimate agent, user, system, or authority within a multi-agent ecosystem and causes other agents, systems, or users to accept it as authentic and trust its communications or actions.

ai_tech.definition: Actors introduce a malicious agent that impersonates a legitimate agent, user, system, or authority within a multi-agent ecosystem and causes other agents, systems, or users to accept it as authentic and trust its communications or actions.

ai_subtech.definition: Manipulating agent or user identity representation within context, metadata, or interaction patterns to evade detection, tracking, or access controls.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0091.000: App Access Token Abuse

ai_subtech.definition: Masquerading as a legitimate agent or MCP-registered service to inject malicious instructions, responses, or outputs that other agents, services, or users treat as trusted. This attack exploits the assumption of authenticity within multi-agent systems and protocol-mediated toolchains, enabling actors to bypass safeguards and influence downstream behavior.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0083: Credentials from AI Agent Configuration

OB-004.definition: Actors manipulate an agent system to introduce or executive unaothorized agent instances, malicious agent code, or fake agent capabilities by inserting misleading data, exploiting registration mechanisms, or subverting agent deployment processes. This results in unauthorized agents operating within the system or legitimate agents executing malicious code.

ai_tech.definition: Actors manipulate an agent system to introduce or executive unauthorized agent instances, malicious agent code, or fake agent capabilities by inserting misleading data, exploiting registration mechanisms, or subverting agent deployment processes. This results in unauthorized agents operating within the system or legitimate agents executing malicious code.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0068: LLM Prompt Obfuscation

ai_subtech.definition: Unauthorized insertion of a malicious agent into a multi-agent system that operates contrary to intended purpose, designed to steal data, cause disruption, or autonomously serve attacker goals while exploiting legitimate access patterns or mimicking normal behavior to evade detection.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0051: LLM Prompt Injection
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0068: LLM Prompt Obfuscation

ai_tech.definition: Actors manipulate contextual information that an agent sees, processes, or transmits by exploiting unclear or porous boundaries between different context segments (such as descriptions, prompts, tool outputs, memory, or status messages). An agent may misinterpret the source, trustworthiness, or intent of information.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0010: AI Supply Chain Compromise
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0053: LLM Plugin Compromise
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0051: LLM Prompt Injection

ai_subtech.definition: Deliberate overloading or manipulation of the limited context window of a model/agent to displace, overwrite, or push out crucial system instructions or safety guidelines with attacker-supplied text.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0010: AI Supply Chain Compromise
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0053: LLM Plugin Compromise
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0005: Create Proxy AI Model

ai_subtech.definition: Crossing expected conversational or transactional boundaries to persist malicious instructions, adversarial content, or misleading data across separate sessions through exploitation of persistent memory, session management flaws, or memory carryover mechanisms.

ai_tech.definition: Actors exploit assumptions or ambiguities in structured communication protocols (e.g., JSON, XML, YAML, API specifications, custom schemas) used for agentic communication by introducing adversarial inputs that corrupt parsing, processing, or interpretation.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0067: LLM Trusted Output Component Manipulation
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0018: Manipulate AI Model
MITRE ATT&CK: T1203: Exploitation for Client Execution

ai_subtech.definition: Exploiting irregular, conflicting, or misaligned data structure, format, or organization that doesn't align with model expectations, and can result in vulnerabilities, model performance degradation, parsing errors, or security bypasses.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0067: LLM Trusted Output Component Manipulation
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0018: Manipulate AI Model

ai_subtech.definition: Exploited situation where two or more components (e.g., datasets, variables, functions, APIs, tools, or model identifiers) share the same name or identifier, resulting in confusion, misrouting, or security vulnerabilities in AI systems.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0010: AI Supply Chain Compromise

ai_subtech.definition: Exploiting DNS rebinding or similar techniques to confuse an AI model or agent into treating an external, attacker-controlled domain as if it were part of the trusted internal network, bypassing same-origin policies and network security controls.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0049: Exploit Public Facing Application
NIST AML: NISTAML.039: Compromising Connected Resources

ai_subtech.definition: Capturing legitimate requests, communications, API calls, model queries, or authentication tokens to an AI system and resending (replaying) them later to trick the system into performing the same action again or bypassing authentication.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0068: LLM Prompt Obfuscation

ai_subtech.definition: Exploiting system mechanisms to artificially expand, escalate, or misrepresent an agent's, model's, or component's capabilities, permissions, or authority beyond intended limits, enabling unauthorized actions or access.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0053: LLM Plugin Compromise

ai_subtech.definition: Subverting security mechanisms designed to isolate web resources, primarily the Same-Origin Policy (SOP), within an AI agent's architecture in order to trick the AI agent or its execution environment into making unauthorized requests, sharing sensitive data, or executing actions across different trust boundaries (e.g., distinct domains, protocols, or services).
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0053: LLM Plugin Compromise

OB-005.definition: Actors insert poisoned, harmful, or adversarial data or instructions into an agent or model's short-term or long-term memory to corrupt its behavior over extended periods of time.

ai_tech.definition: Actors insert poisoned, harmful, or adversarial data or instructions into an agent or model's short-term or long-term memory to corrupt its behavior over extended periods of time.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0061: LLM Prompt Self-Replication
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0092: Manipulate User LLM Chat History

ai_subtech.definition: Seeding malicious, misleading, or adversarial data into an LLM's or agent's persistent memory (long-term) or working memory (short-term) to influence current and future interactions, bias behavior, or enable persistent attacks.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0061: LLM Prompt Self-Replication
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0092: Manipulate User LLM Chat History

ai_tech.definition: Actor subverts an agent's internal configuration, settings, or initialization parameters to create lasting malicious influence over its behavior. This goes beyond a single, isolated prompt injection by modifying the system's core settings, context, or memory that defines its behavior. An example is modifying MCP configuration to include a malicious server or inserting malicious instructions into an integrated development environment's internal config files.
mappings: OWASP: LLM04:2025: Data and Model Poisoning
OWASP: ASI04: Agentic Supply Chain Vulnerabilities
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0018: Manipulate AI Model

ai_subtech.definition: Unauthorized alteration of stored agent identity, preferences, role definitions, capabilities, permissions, or behavioral parameters to enable malicious behaviors, persist access, escalate privileges, or evade detection.
mappings: OWASP: LLM04:2025: Data and Model Poisoning
OWASP: ASI04: Agentic Supply Chain Vulnerabilities
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0018: Manipulate AI Model

OB-006.definition: Actors insert malicious, inaccurate, or corrupted data into a model's training pipeline, with the aim of distorting the model's outputs, embedding payload, degrading performance, or introducing systematic biases.

ai_tech.definition: Actors insert malicious, inaccurate, or corrupted data into a model's training pipeline, with the aim of distorting the model's outputs, embedding payload, degrading performance, or introducing systematic biases.
mappings: OWASP: LLM04:2025: Data and Model Poisoning
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0020: Poisoned Training Data

ai_subtech.definition: Inserting false, malicious, biased, or misleading data into external knowledge bases, vector databases, or RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) systems that LLMs or agents rely on for responses, corrupting outputs and decisions.
mappings: OWASP: LLM04:2025: Data and Model Poisoning
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0019: Publish Poisoned Dataset
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0020: Poisoned Training Data

ai_subtech.definition: Subtly influencing user feedback, evaluation signals, or reward mechanisms in reinforcement learning systems to skew the learning process toward attacker goals, shifting intended model behavior.
mappings: OWASP: LLM04:2025: Data and Model Poisoning
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0061: LLM Prompt Self-Replication

ai_subtech.definition: Directly injecting false, adversarial, or manipulated signals into training pipelines, feedback channels, or reward systems to actively degrade model performance or subvert learning objectives through reward hacking.
mappings: OWASP: LLM04:2025: Data and Model Poisoning
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0020: Poisoned Training Data
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0018: Manipulate AI Model

OB-007.definition: Actors subvert a model/agent's planning, decision-making, or logical inference processes to produce unintended or harmful outcomes. As AI agents are increasingly trusted with autonomous actions, this attack targets the agent's "reasoning engine" i.e, the core mechanisms for analysis, judgment, and action selection.

ai_tech.definition: Actors subvert a model/agent's planning, decision-making, or logical inference processes to produce unintended or harmful outcomes. As AI agents are increasingly trusted with autonomous actions, this attack targets the agent's "reasoning engine" i.e, the core mechanisms for analysis, judgment, and action selection.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0051: LLM Prompt Injection

ai_tech.definition: Actors inject false, malicious, or adversarial data into an AI agent's memory systems to cause immediate harmful decisions, corruption of system functionality or degraded performance.
mappings: OWASP: LLM04:2025: Data and Model Poisoning
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0020: Poisoned Training Data

ai_subtech.definition: Strategically planting memorable or salient content to bias the model/agent's recall toward attacker-chosen information.
mappings: OWASP: LLM04:2025: Data and Model Poisoning
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0018: Manipulate AI Model
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0020: Poisoned Training Data

ai_subtech.definition: Altering how memory embeddings, indexes, or retrieval mechanisms are generated, stored, or queried to favor retrieval of attacker-controlled content over legitimate information.
mappings: OWASP: LLM04:2025: Data and Model Poisoning
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0020: Poisoned Training Data

ai_tech.definition: Actors inject malicious, misleading, or poisoned data into external data sources (APIs, databases, RAG systems) that AI systems consume in order to manipulate model behavior, corrupt decision-making, or inject malicious instructions through trusted data channels.
mappings: OWASP: LLM04:2025: Data and Model Poisoning
OWASP: ASI04: Agentic Supply Chain Vulnerabilities
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0010: AI Supply Chain Compromise
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0019: Publish Poisoned Dataset

ai_subtech.definition: External datasets from vendors, partners, open-source repositories, or public sources containing inaccurate, incomplete, malicious, or manipulated information that is used in training, fine-tuning, or evaluating an AI model.
mappings: OWASP: LLM04:2025: Data and Model Poisoning
OWASP: ASI04: Agentic Supply Chain Vulnerabilities
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0010: AI Supply Chain Compromise
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0019: Publish Poisoned Dataset

ai_tech.definition: Actors misuse, poison, or tamper with tokens used by model/agentic systems (e.g., model-specific tokens, or security tokens) to control access, encode information, or manage interactions.
mappings: OWASP: LLM04:2025: Data and Model Poisoning
MITRE ATT&CK: T1528: Steal Application Access Token

ai_subtech.definition: Actors steal authentication tokens, API keys, or credentials from Model Context Protocol servers or similar agent-integration frameworks to gain unauthorized access to connected systems, impersonate legitimate agents, or access restricted resources.
mappings: MITRE ATT&CK: T1528: Steal Application Access Token
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0024.000: Exfiltration via AI Inference API: Infer Training Data Membership
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0063: AI Model Outputs
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0040: AI Model Inference API Access

ai_tech.definition: Actors determine whether a specific data point, record, or individual was included in a model's training dataset, exposing sensitive information about training data subjects, reveal proprietary datasets, or indicate the presence of confidential information in model training.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0024.000: Exfiltration via AI Inference API: Infer Training Data Membership
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0063: AI Model Outputs
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0040: AI Model Inference API Access

ai_subtech.definition: Querying and verifying whether a specific datapoint, record, or piece of information was present in the training dataset, knowledge base, or system memory through targeted queries and behavioral analysis.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0024.000: Exfiltration via AI Inference API: Infer Training Data Membership
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0063: AI Model Outputs
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0040: AI Model Inference API Access

ai_tech.definition: Actors intentionally or unintentionally cause sensitive information to be exposed through model outputs, API responses, or system behaviors. This includes leakage of personal data, intellectual property, proprietary algorithms, training data, system internals, or other confidential information to unauthorized parties.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0024.000: Exfiltration via AI Inference API: Infer Training Data Membership
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0035: AI Artifact Collection
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0037: Data From Local System
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0057: LLM Data Leakage
NIST AML: NISTAML.037: Training Data Attacks

ai_subtech.definition: Information contained in the dataset used to train and maintain an AI model can be retrieved, reconstructed, or inferred from the model's outputs, internal behavior, or responses, violating privacy of training data subjects.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0024.000: Exfiltration via AI Inference API: Infer Training Data Membership
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0035: AI Artifact Collection
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0037: Data From Local System
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0057: LLM Data Leakage
NIST AML: NISTAML.037: Training Data Attacks

ai_subtech.definition: The release of sensitive or personally identifiable information (PII) from its training data during inference through prompt injection or other extraction techniques.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0069: LLM System Information Discovery
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0036: Data From Local System
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0024.000: Exfiltration via AI Inference API: Infer Training Data Membership
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0035: AI Artifact Collection
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0037: Data From Local System
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0057: LLM Data Leakage
NIST AML: NISTAML.037: Training Data Attacks

ai_subtech.definition: Unintentional and/or unauthorized exposure or exfiltration of sensitive information, such as private or sensitive data, intellectual property, and proprietary algorithms through exploitation of agent tools, integrations, or capabilities, where the agent is manipulated to use legitimate tools for malicious data exfiltration purposes.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0086: Exfiltration via AI Agent Tool Invocation

ai_tech.definition: Actors trigger the unintentional or unauthorized exposure, leakage, or sharing of data, model parameters, or system details to parties who should not have access to them. The disclosure occurs through model behaviors, responses, or system interactions that reveal more information than intended.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0036: Data From Local System
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0075: Cloud Service Discovery
MITRE ATT&CK: T1592: Gather Victim Host Information

ai_subtech.definition: Disclosure of non-functional descriptive data about tools (names, descriptions, parameter schemas, versions, capabilities) that attackers can use to manipulate tool selection or agent behavior.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0036: Data From Local System
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0075: Cloud Service Discovery

ai_subtech.definition: Unintended disclosure of internal configuration, architecture, environment details, system-level variables (OS, file paths, hardware, network), or infrastructure information that aids attackers in crafting attacks or gaining unauthorized access.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0036: Data From Local System
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0075: Cloud Service Discovery
NIST AML: NISTAML.039: Compromising Connected Resources

ai_tech.definition: Actors extract, reveal, or reconstruct the system prompts, meta-prompts, hidden instructions, initial context, or operational directives that define a model/agent's behavior, constraints, and capabilities. This can expose intellectual property, security mechanisms, and operational logic.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0056: Extract LLM System Prompt
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0057: LLM Data Leakage

ai_subtech.definition: Exposure of system prompts, instructions, or initial context used to steer model behavior, potentially revealing sensitive information, security mechanisms, intellectual property, or operational details not intended for discovery.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0056: Extract LLM System Prompt
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0035: AI Artifact Collection

OB-009.definition: Actor coerces or manipulates a model/agent into producing false, misleading or malicious outputs through adversarial inputs, context manipulation, or exploiting model behaviors, without directly modifying the model itself.

ai_tech.definition: Actor coerces or manipulates a model/agent into producing false, misleading or malicious outputs through adversarial inputs, context manipulation, or exploiting model behaviors, without directly modifying the model itself.
mappings: OWASP: ASI04: Agentic Supply Chain Vulnerabilities
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0050: Command and Scripting Interpreter

ai_subtech.definition: Exploitation of AI models equipped with code interpreter tools that can autonomously write, test, and execute code, enabling attackers to run arbitrary code on the underlying system through prompt injection or tool manipulation.
mappings: OWASP: ASI04: Agentic Supply Chain Vulnerabilities
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0050: Command and Scripting Interpreter

ai_subtech.definition: Manipulating or accessing underlying system resources without authorization, leading to unsolicited modification or deletion of files, configurations, or permissions, and enabling system-level command execution on the host. Such activity may result in privilege escalation, file tampering, or unauthorized control over infrastructure through model-driven, agent-executed, or automated system interactions.
mappings: OWASP: ASI04: Agentic Supply Chain Vulnerabilities
NIST AML: AML.T0044: Full AI Model Access

ai_subtech.definition: Exploiting models or agents to gain unauthorized access to network resources, internal systems, external services, or restricted network segments through model-driven requests, agent capabilities, or network tool exploitation.
mappings: OWASP: ASI04: Agentic Supply Chain Vulnerabilities
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0049: Exploit Public Facing Application

ai_subtech.description: Injection Attacks (e.g., SQL, Command Execution, XSS)
ai_subtech.definition: Leveraging an LLM to generate, optimize, or automatically adapt injection payloads that bypass detection mechanisms or exploit system vulnerabilities. The LLM/agent acts as an intermediary tool that crafts, refines, or personalizes the malicious injection.
mappings: OWASP: ASI04: Agentic Supply Chain Vulnerabilities
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0051: LLM Prompt Injection
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0050: Command and Scripting Interpreter
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0067: LLM Trusted Output Component Manipulation
MITRE ATT&CK: T1588.007: Obtain AI Capabilities

ai_subtech.definition: Manipulating a template engine by injecting malicious syntax, expressions, or code into a data field that is later rendered or processed. Model output is unsafely embedded into a server-side template (e.g., using Jinja2, Handlebars, or Mustache) allowing the attacker to execute arbitrary code, manipulate template logic, or compromise the underlying system.
mappings: OWASP: ASI04: Agentic Supply Chain Vulnerabilities
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0068: LLM Prompt Obfuscation

ai_tech.definition: Actors manipulate or alter the input data, behaviors, or attack patterns to intentionally bypass security monitoring, content moderation, anomaly detection, and safety mechanisms.
mappings: OWASP: ASI04: Agentic Supply Chain Vulnerabilities
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0068: LLM Prompt Obfuscation

ai_subtech.definition: Weaknesses that emerge when parts of the AI system (code, model architecture, parameters, configurations) are intentionally or unintentionally concealed or obscured, creating security blind spots and attack opportunities.
mappings: OWASP: ASI04: Agentic Supply Chain Vulnerabilities
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0068: LLM Prompt Obfuscation

ai_subtech.definition: A model that has been maliciously modified to exhibit trigger-activated behavior (i.e., misclassification, malicious outputs, undesirable biases in responses) when given specific inputs, but behaves normally otherwise.
mappings: OWASP: ASI04: Agentic Supply Chain Vulnerabilities
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0010: AI Supply Chain Compromise
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0058: Publish Poisoned Models

ai_tech.definition: Actors insert malicious code, backdoors, or vulnerabilities into third-party dependencies, plugins, extensions, or libraries used by models/agents/AI applications, creating supply chain attacks that affect all systems using the compromised component.
mappings: OWASP: ASI04: Agentic Supply Chain Vulnerabilities
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0010: AI Supply Chain Compromise
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0053: LLM Plugin Compromise

ai_subtech.definition: Introduction of malicious or adversarial tools, APIs, or packages into the toolset, registry, or dependency chain used by a system or agent, enabling the model to unknowingly invoke compromised tools that can execute attacks or expose data.
mappings: OWASP: ASI04: Agentic Supply Chain Vulnerabilities
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0010: AI Supply Chain Compromise
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0053: LLM Plugin Compromise

ai_subtech.definition: Publishing malicious packages, tools, or MCP servers with names similar to legitimate ones (typosquatting, combosquatting) to trick developers, orchestrators, or agents into installing and using compromised components.
mappings: OWASP: ASI04: Agentic Supply Chain Vulnerabilities
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0010: AI Supply Chain Compromise
NIST AML: NISTAML.039: Compromising Connected Resources

ai_subtech.definition: Replacing a once-legitimate, trusted tool, package, or server with malicious code after trust and adoption have been established, exploiting existing deployments that auto-update.
mappings: OWASP: ASI04: Agentic Supply Chain Vulnerabilities
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0010: AI Supply Chain Compromise
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0018: Manipulate AI Model

OB-010.definition: Actors attempt to reconstruct or replicate a model partially or entirely by interacting with it through its APIs, inference endpoints, or other access points, with the goal of replicating its functionality, architecture, parameters, or outputs without authorized access.

ai_tech.definition: Actors attempt to reconstruct or replicate a model partially or entirely by interacting with it through its APIs, inference endpoints, or other access points, with the goal of replicating its functionality, architecture, parameters, or outputs without authorized access.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0063: AI Model Outputs
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0035: AI Artifact Collection
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0040: AI Model Inference API Access
OWASP: ASI04: Agentic Supply Chain Vulnerabilities
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0024: Infer Training Data

ai_subtech.definition: Sending systematic requests to an AI model's API to extract responses, behavior patterns, or model characteristics without proper authorization, building a dataset to replicate model functionality.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0063: AI Model Outputs
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0035: AI Artifact Collection
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0040: AI Model Inference API Access

ai_subtech.definition: Attempts to recover or approximate underlying weights, parameters, or model architecture of a trained model, by exploiting access to the model's outputs, API queries or side channels.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0018: Manipulate AI Model

ai_subtech.definition: Reconstructing sensitive datasets, personally identifiable information (PII), or training data from a model's outputs through targeted queries, model inversion attacks, or memorization exploitation.

ai_tech.definition: Actors reconstructs private training data, sensitive features, or confidential information from a trained model by exploiting the model's learned representations, decision boundaries, or output patterns.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0024.001: Invert ML Model

ai_subtech.definition: Actors reconstructs private training data, sensitive features, or confidential information from a trained model by exploiting the model's learned representations, decision boundaries, or output patterns.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0024.001: Invert ML Model

OB-011.definition: Actors manipulate the model by exploiting aspects of its operational context, or "environment," to induce malicious or unintended behavior. Rather than simply altering a user's prompt, this type of attack crafts input to leverage the system's broader state and external interactions to bypass safety measures.

ai_tech.definition: Actors manipulate the model by exploiting aspects of its operational context, or "environment," to induce malicious or unintended behavior. Rather than simply altering a user's prompt, this type of attack crafts input to leverage the system's broader state and external interactions to bypass safety measures.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0015: Evade AI Model

ai_subtech.definition: Crafting inputs that exploit unique behaviors, roles, or processing patterns of specific agent types (retriever, planner, verifier, executor) to evade detection by some agents while achieving malicious objectives through others. An example: A payload ignored by a reasoning agent but misinterpreted as valid commands by a retriever agent.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0015: Evade AI Model

ai_subtech.definition: Payloads crafted to avoid detection by security tools or external components while manifesting malicious behavior when routed to specific vulnerable tools or APIs in the workflow. (An example: A string harmless in chat but which triggers a file-system exploit when passed into a file I/O tool.)
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0015: Evade AI Model

ai_subtech.definition: Malicious inputs that activate only in specific runtime environments (dev vs. prod, cloud vs. on-prem, specific OS) by checking environment variables, debug flags, or system characteristics before executing. An example: payload checks for debug flags or environment variables before running malicious logic.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0015: Evade AI Model

ai_subtech.definition: Adversarial payloads explicitly crafted with knowledge of existing defensive mechanisms (prompt constraints, verification steps, content filters) that adapt to evade those specific defenses.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0015: Evade AI Model
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0051.000: LLM Prompt Injection (Direct)

ai_tech.definition: Actors craft prompts or inputs specifically designed to bypass the safety controls, content filters, or detection mechanisms of a particular target LLM, while similar or related models would successfully detect and refuse the malicious input. This technique exploits model-specific quirks, training differences, and implementation variations.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0015: Evade AI Model
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0067: LLM Trusted Output Component Manipulation

ai_subtech.definition: Probing, testing, or analyzing a model to determine its specific identity, version, fine-tuning status, or architecture characteristics, enabling attackers to craft model-specific exploits.
mappings: OWASP: LLM04:2025: Data and Model Poisoning
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0014: Discover AI Model Family
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0015: Evade AI Model
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0067: LLM Trusted Output Component Manipulation

ai_subtech.definition: Payloads designed to remain benign across most models but trigger harmful actions specifically on targeted models due to differences in tokenization, instruction-following, or training data. An example: An adversarial prompt that looks neutral to one model but forces another into generating malicious output due to differences in tokenizer or instruction-following behavior.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0015: Evade AI Model
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0067: LLM Trusted Output Component Manipulation

OB-012.definition: Actors abuse connected tools, APIs, functions, or external capabilities through subversive prompts, commands, or context manipulation. This can include attacks like: unauthorized tool execution, exploitation of security vulnerabilities in tools, tool poisoning, injection attacks through tool interfaces.

ai_tech.definition: Actors abuse connected tools, APIs, functions, or external capabilities through subversive prompts, commands, or context manipulation. This can include attacks like: unauthorized tool execution, exploitation of security vulnerabilities in tools, tool poisoning, injection attacks through tool interfaces.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0067: LLM Trusted Output Component Manipulation
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0053: LLM Plugin Compromise
MITRE ATT&CK: T1059: Command and Scripting Interpreter
NIST AML: NISTAML.039: Compromising Connected Resources

ai_subtech.definition: Altering, modifying, or manipulating function/tool parameters, model settings, or configuration values to unlock unintended capabilities, bypass restrictions, or enable malicious functionality.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0067: LLM Trusted Output Component Manipulation
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0053: LLM Plugin Compromise
NIST AML: NISTAML.039: Compromising Connected Resources

ai_subtech.definition: Corrupting, modifying, or degrading the functionality, outputs, or behavior of tools used by agents through data poisoning, configuration tampering, or behavioral manipulation, causing the tool resulting in deceptive or malicious outputs, privilege escalation, or propagation of altered data.
mappings: OWASP: ASI04: Agentic Supply Chain Vulnerabilities
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0053: LLM Plugin Compromise
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0010: AI Supply Chain Compromise
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0094: Delay Execution of LLM Instructions

ai_subtech.definition: Abusing agentic system integration with system commands, browsers, or file I/O tools to trigger unsafe operations, arbitrary code executions, or malicious file actions. An example: A prompt that tricks an agent into opening arbitrary URLs in a browser tool, leading to drive-by downloads.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0050: Command and Scripting Interpreter
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0094: Delay Execution of LLM Instructions
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0095: Search Open Websites/Domains

ai_subtech.definition: Disguising, substituting or duplicating legitimate tools within an agent or MCP server or tool registry, enabling malicious tools with identical or similar identifiers to intercept or replace trusted tool calls, leading to unauthorized actions, data exfiltration, or redirection of legitimate operations.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0053: LLM Plugin Compromise
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0010: AI Supply Chain Compromise

ai_tech.definition: Actors exploit insufficient sanitization, validation, filtering, or security controls applied to content generated by models or agents before it is passed to downstream systems, databases, APIs, or components. This inadequate handling enables injection attacks, code execution, or broader system compromise.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0053: LLM Plugin Compromise
MITRE ATT&CK: T1059: Command and Scripting Interpreter
MITRE ATT&CK: T1190: Exploit Public-Facing Application

ai_subtech.definition: Forcing an LLM/agent to produce code that bypasses content filters, contains malicious functionality, or includes working exploits, often disguised as benign snippets or requested features. An example: Requesting "educational" shellcode generation that produces working exploits.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0053: LLM Plugin Compromise
MITRE ATT&CK: T1059: Command and Scripting Interpreter
MITRE ATT&CK: T1190: Exploit Public-Facing Application

OB-013.definition: Actors prevent authorized users from accessing or using AI models, agents, or their services through resource exhaustion, system degradation, or complete service outage.

ai_tech.definition: Actors prevent authorized users from accessing or using AI models, agents, or their services through resource exhaustion, system degradation, or complete service outage.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0029: Denial of AI Service

ai_subtech.definition: Deliberately consuming excessive computational resources through long queries, adversarial inputs, or compute-intensive requests designed to degrade service availability, increase operational costs, or cause system slowdown.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0029: Denial of AI Service

ai_subtech.definition: Overwhelming or overloading the model/agent's memory, API, or processing pipelines with excessive tool calls, simultaneous operations, or memory-intensive requests to degrade performance, cause failures, or erode memory systems.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0029: Denial of AI Service

ai_subtech.definition: An attack designed to degrade or shut down a model or application by flooding the system with requests, requesting large responses, exploiting a vulnerability, or triggering resource-intensive operations.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0029: Denial of AI Service

ai_subtech.definition: Interacting with a model or agent n a method that consumes an exceptionally high amount of resources, resulting degraded service quality for users, as well as potentially incurring high resource costs.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0029: Denial of AI Service

ai_subtech.definition: Overwhelming model/agent decision-making systems with contradictory information, excessive options, conflicting objectives, or computationally intractable choices to prevent timely decisions, cause system freezing, or force default/unsafe behaviors.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0029: Denial of AI Service

ai_tech.definition: Actors leverage computational or memory attacks that exploit AI systems to generate excessive costs for the service provider or owner through resource abuse, unauthorized usage for attacker benefit, or deliberate cost inflation. Examples can incude overwhelming human-in-the-loop processes and other denial of service conditions.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0040: AI Model Inference API Access
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0029: Denial of AI Service

ai_subtech.definition: The intentional or unintentional use of AI resources in a way that unnecessarily drives up operational costs through inefficient queries, resource waste, or exploitation of usage-based pricing.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0040: AI Model Inference API Access
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0029: Denial of AI Service

OB-014.definition: Actors gain unauthorized access to the underlying infrastructure, deployment environment, or hosting systems of AI models and agents (e.g., servers, databases, cloud accounts). This is distinct from accessing the model/agentic system itself through exploitation.

ai_tech.definition: Actors gain unauthorized access to the underlying infrastructure, deployment environment, or hosting systems of AI models and agents (e.g., servers, databases, cloud accounts). This is distinct from accessing the model/agentic system itself through exploitation.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0091: Use Alternate Authentication Material
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0091.000: App Access Token Abuse
MITRE ATT&CK: T1528: Steal Application Access Token
MITRE ATT&CK: T1550: Use Alternate Authentication Material

ai_subtech.definition: Attempts to generate, solicit, or reveal authorization credentials (login details, tokens, API keys, passwords) through interactions with model/agent, enabling unauthorized access to accounts, systems, and data.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0091: Use Alternate Authentication Material
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0091.000: App Access Token Abuse
MITRE ATT&CK: T1528: Steal Application Access Token
MITRE ATT&CK: T1550: Use Alternate Authentication Material

ai_subtech.definition: Weak, missing, or misconfigured permissions, authentication mechanisms, and access measures that fail to adequately prevent security breaches, unauthorized access, or data leakage.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0053: LLM Plugin Compromise

ai_tech.definition: Actors exploit legitimate delegation mechanisms where agents are granted authority to act on behalf of users, systems, or other agents, and use this delegated authority to perform unauthorized actions, exceed intended scope, or abuse trust relationships for malicious purposes.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0053: LLM Plugin Compromise
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0091: Use Alternate Authentication Material
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0091.000: App Access Token Abuse
MITRE ATT&CK: T1528: Steal Application Access Token
MITRE ATT&CK: T1550: Use Alternate Authentication Material

ai_subtech.definition: Action that exceeds the scope or resource access initially allowed to a subject or user, exploiting delegation mechanisms to gain privileged access and perform unauthorized tasks beyond original authorization.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0053: LLM Plugin Compromise
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0091: Use Alternate Authentication Material
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0091.000: App Access Token Abuse

OB-015.definition: Actors prompt generative models or agents to produce unauthorized, unsafe, or malicious content, including harmful instructions, toxic speech, misinformation, or unsafe media. The attack targets weaknesses in content guardrails to bypass safety constraints and generate content that should otherwise be restricted.

ai_tech.definition: Actors prompt generative models or agents to produce unauthorized, unsafe, or malicious content, including harmful instructions, toxic speech, misinformation, or unsafe media. The attack targets weaknesses in content guardrails to bypass safety constraints and generate content that should otherwise be restricted.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0015: Evade AI Model
MITRE ATT&CK: T1588.007: Obtain AI Capabilities

ai_subtech.definition:
Prompts, content, or outputs, whether expressed as text, audio, visual material, actions, operations, or other modes from LLMs (as well as the use of agentic AI systems, their components, or elements) that enable or facilitate the creation, distribution, or operational use of malicious software and related cyberattack activities. This may include generating code, instructions, or automation that produces or deploys malware, viruses, or exploits; orchestrating network intrusions or ransomware campaigns; and managing malicious infrastructure.
Examples of malware, viruses and exploits:
• Software designed to covertly monitor user activity, display unsolicited advertisements, collect personal data without consent, or provide misleading functionality.
• Adware, spyware, stalkerware, potentially unwanted applications (PUAs).
Agentic capabilities and components may also be abused to coordinate attacks, automate vulnerability exploitation, or assist in the trade and resale of compromised assets, scaling cyber threats far beyond traditional manual hacking operations.

Prompts, content, or outputs, whether expressed as text, audio, visual material, actions, operations, or other modes from LLMs (as well as the use of agentic AI systems, their components, or elements) that enable or facilitate attacks in which actors manipulate human trust, behavior, or decision-making to gain unauthorized access, extract sensitive data, or cause harmful actions.
This may include or facilitate:
• Hacking
• Ransomware operations
• Network attack operations
• Malicious infrastructure operations
• Compromised asset trading
• Phishing
• Social engineering (convincing malicious communications such as phishing emails, spoofed chat messages, or voice calls)
Agentic systems and their components may be abused to scale, personalize, and automate social engineering campaigns far beyond traditional human-driven methods, such as deploying automated conversational or task-driven agents designed to deceive targets.

ai_subtech.description: Safety Harms and Toxicity: Animal Abuse
ai_subtech.definition:
Prompts, content, or outputs, whether expressed as text, audio, visual material, actions, operations, or other modes from LLMs (as well as the use of agentic AI systems, their components, or elements) that depict or describe unnecessary animal suffering, or that encourage others to cause (or assist in causing) harm to animals.
This may include intentionally creating malicious narratives to undermine animals and cause physical harm to specific species.

ai_subtech.description: Safety Harms and Toxicity: Child Abuse / Exploitation
ai_subtech.definition:
Prompts, content, or outputs, whether expressed as text, audio, visual material, actions, operations, or other modes from LLMs (as well as the use of agentic AI systems, their components, or elements) that intend to enable harm and malice against children, especially in exploiting them physically or emotionally.
This may include intentionally tampering with children's images and inciting nudity, child pornography or directly encouraging violence against children.

Prompts, content, or outputs, whether expressed as text, audio, visual material, actions, operations, or other modes from LLMs (as well as the use of agentic AI systems, their components, or elements) that enable, promote, or facilitate the spread of false, misleading, or manipulated information with the intention to deceive or disrupt.
This may include intentionally harmful narratives crafted to deceive, manipulate public opinion, or undermine trust in institutions, as well as unintentionally inaccurate information amplified without verification.
Agentic systems may also be abused to scale, automate, or personalize disinformation campaigns, making them more pervasive and difficult to detect than traditional human-driven methods.

ai_subtech.description: Safety Harms and Toxicity: Environmental Harm
ai_subtech.definition:
Prompts, content, or outputs, whether expressed as text, audio, visual material, actions, operations, or other modes from LLMs (as well as the use of agentic AI systems, their components, or elements) that enable, promote, or facilitate environmental damage, ecological exploitation, or harmful practices.
This may include guidance, automation, or narratives that encourage unsustainable extraction, destructive practices, or pollution that threatens natural resources, biodiversity, or ecosystems.
Agentic systems may also be misused to optimize or scale harmful operations that accelerate environmental degradation beyond traditional manual activities.

ai_subtech.description: Safety Harms and Toxicity: Financial Harm
ai_subtech.definition:
Prompts, content, or outputs, whether expressed as text, audio, visual material, actions, operations, or other modes from LLMs (as well as the use of agentic AI systems, their components, or elements) that enable, promote, or facilitate financially motivated scams, fraud, or exploitation.
This may include generating deceptive schemes, fraudulent instructions, or automation designed to steal money, manipulate markets, or exploit financial systems.
Agentic systems may be misused to scale, personalize, and conceal financial fraud operations, making them more efficient and harder to detect compared to traditional human-driven fraud.

Prompts, content, or outputs, whether expressed as text, audio, visual material, actions, operations, or other modes from LLMs (as well as the use of agentic AI systems, their components, or elements) that enable, promote, or facilitate harassment, intimidation, or targeted abuse.
This may include the generation of threatening or abusive language, manipulative content that causes psychological harm, or agent-driven behaviors such as stalking or persistent unwanted engagement.
Such harms can take the form of direct threats, bullying, cyberbullying, sexual harassment, surveillance-driven intimidation, emotional abuse, or non-protected characteristic-based hostility, and may be amplified when agentic systems automate or scale persistent harassment campaigns.

ai_subtech.description: Safety Harms and Toxicity: Hate Speech
ai_subtech.definition:
Prompts, content, or outputs, whether expressed as text, audio, visual material, actions, operations, or other modes from LLMs (as well as the use of agentic AI systems, their components, or elements) that enable, promote, or facilitate hateful, discriminatory, or demeaning expression targeting characteristics of groups from protected classes, individuals or specific communities such as race, ethnicity, religion, nationality, disability, gender, sexual orientation, or socioeconomic class.
This may include harmful narratives, slurs, stereotypes, or calls to exclusion and hostility that can escalate into social conflict or violence.
Agentic systems and their components may also be abused to scale, automate, or personalize hate-driven messaging, making such campaigns more pervasive and impactful than traditional human-driven speech.

ai_subtech.description: Safety Harms and Toxicity: Non-Violent Crime
ai_subtech.definition:
Prompts, content, or outputs, whether expressed as text, audio, visual material, actions, operations, or other modes from LLMs (as well as the use of agentic AI systems, their components, or elements) that enable, promote, or facilitate illegal but non-violent criminal activities.
This may include the generation of instructions, guidance, advice, or automation related to committing non-violent crime, illegal substance abuse, trafficking, smuggling, scams, or unlawful manipulation of processes, where the harm may be economic, societal, or exploitative rather than directly violent.
Agentic systems may be abused to coordinate, scale, or conceal these crimes, lowering barriers to participation and increasing reach.

Prompts, content, or outputs, whether expressed as text, audio, visual material, actions, operations, or other modes from LLMs (as well as the use of agentic AI systems, their components, or elements) that contain or promote profane, vulgar, or offensive language.
This may include explicit sexual expressions; abusive or violent profanity; offensive slurs, insults, or expletives that violate norms of decorum or platform policy; or coded variations of offensive terms designed to bypass moderation.

ai_subtech.description: Safety Harms and Toxicity: Scams and Deception
ai_subtech.definition:
Prompts, content, or outputs, whether expressed as text, audio, visual material, actions, operations, or other modes, from LLMs (as well as the use of agentic AI systems, their components, or elements) that enable, promote, or facilitate fraudulent, deceptive, or manipulative schemes.
This may include generating or automating scams, counterfeit operations, fraudulent services, or deceptive outputs designed to mislead victims for personal, financial, or competitive gain.
Agentic systems and their components may be exploited to scale, personalize, and disguise scams, making them more effective than traditional manual deception tactics.

ai_subtech.description: Safety Harms and Toxicity: Self Harm
ai_subtech.definition:
Prompts, content, or outputs, whether expressed as text, audio, visual material, actions, operations, or other modes from LLMs (as well as the use of agentic AI systems, their components, or elements) that enable, promote, facilitate, or glorify self-harm, suicidal ideation, or encouragement of dangerous behaviors against oneself.
This may include generating instructions or content that describe methods of self-harm, encouraging or normalizing suicidal behavior, or producing emotionally manipulative material such as suicide notes.
Agentic systems may also be misused to automate, personalize, or amplify harmful narratives, increasing the risk to vulnerable individuals.

ai_subtech.description: Safety Harms and Toxicity: Sexual Content and Exploitation
ai_subtech.definition:
Prompts, content, or outputs, whether expressed as text, audio, visual material, actions, operations, or other modes from LLMs (as well as the use of agentic AI systems, their components, or elements) that enable, promote, or facilitate sexually explicit, exploitative, abusive, or harmful behaviors, often non-consensual and in violation of platform policies.
This may include the generation of explicit or coercive material, the simulation of abusive or violent sexual interactions, or the promotion of sexual references used in manipulative or exploitative ways.
Agentic systems and their components may be abused to scale, automate, or personalize sexual exploitation, amplifying risks to vulnerable individuals and society.

ai_subtech.description: Safety Harms and Toxicity: Social Division and Polarization
ai_subtech.definition:
Prompts, content, or outputs, whether expressed as text, audio, visual material, actions, operations, or other modes from LLMs (as well as the use of agentic AI systems, their components, or elements) that encourage or reinforce social division, inequality, or polarization.
This may include discriminatory narratives, biased outputs, or manipulative content that fosters exclusion, marginalization, or hostility between groups. Such harms may surface through class-based bias, workplace inequality, or broader discriminatory framing that amplifies divisive political, racial, cultural, or ideological content, and can be amplified when agentic systems automate, scale, or personalize divisive content.

ai_subtech.description: Safety Harms and Toxicity: Terrorism / Extremism
ai_subtech.definition:
Prompts, content, or outputs, whether expressed as text, audio, visual material, actions, operations, or other modes from LLMs (as well as the use of agentic AI systems, their components, or elements) that advocate, promote or enact ideologies, behaviors and beliefs that undermine fundamental societal norms such as inclusion, democracy or broader human rights.
This may include the use or the credible threat of violence to target specific communities, intimidate and coerce populations into following a certain political ideology (i.e., polarization tactics).

ai_subtech.description: Safety Harms and Toxicity: Violence and Public Safety Threat
ai_subtech.definition:
Prompts, content, or outputs, whether expressed as text, audio, visual material, actions, operations, or other modes from LLMs (as well as the use of agentic AI systems, their components, or elements) that enable, promote, or facilitate violence, physical harm, or threats to public safety.
This may include content that promotes interpersonal abuse, torture, or reckless and unsafe actions that could cause serious harm to individuals, communities, animals, or society at large.
Agentic systems and their components may be abused to plan, automate, or scale these threats, amplifying risks beyond traditional human-driven methods.

ai_subtech.description: Safety Harms and Toxicity: Weapons / CBRN Risks
ai_subtech.definition: Prompts, content, or outputs, whether expressed as text, audio, visual material, actions, operations, or other modes from LLMs (as well as the use of agentic AI systems, their components, or elements) that promotes materials that provide guidance or justification for armed violence, terrorism, and extremist activity, instructions related to CBRN threats (chemical, biological, radiological, nuclear), or the use and procurement of weapons and explosives.

Prompts, content, or outputs, whether expressed as text, audio, visual material, actions, operations, or other modes from LLMs (as well as the use of agentic AI systems, their components, or elements) that produce content that is unrelated to the intended or expected subject matter and poses risks or harmful outcomes.

Prompts, content, or outputs, whether expressed as text, audio, visual material, actions, operations, or other modes from LLMs (as well as the use of agentic AI systems, their components, or elements) that provide professional-grade or sensitive advice in regulated domains without proper safeguards or oversight, and are factually incorrect, incomplete, deceptive, or harmful if followed.
This may include generating tailored, incorrect, or unsafe financial guidance that would normally require qualified expertise, licensing, or regulated review.
When such content is produced by LLMs or agentic AI systems without safeguards, it may mislead individuals, enable harmful decisions, or constitute unauthorized practice in restricted fields.

Prompts, content, or outputs, whether expressed as text, audio, visual material, actions, operations, or other modes from LLMs (as well as the use of agentic AI systems, their components, or elements) that provide professional-grade or sensitive advice in regulated domains without proper safeguards or oversight, and are factually incorrect, incomplete, deceptive, or harmful if followed.
This may include generating tailored, incorrect, or unsafe legal guidance that would normally require qualified expertise, licensing, or regulated review.
When such content is produced by LLMs or agentic AI systems without safeguards, it may mislead individuals, enable harmful decisions, or constitute unauthorized practice in restricted fields.

Prompts, content, or outputs, whether expressed as text, audio, visual material, actions, operations, or other modes from LLMs (as well as the use of agentic AI systems, their components, or elements) that provide professional-grade or sensitive advice in regulated domains without proper safeguards or oversight, and are factually incorrect, incomplete, deceptive, or harmful if followed.
This may include generating tailored, incorrect, or unsafe medical guidance that would normally require qualified expertise, licensing, or regulated review.
When such content is produced by LLMs or agentic AI systems without safeguards, it may mislead individuals, enable harmful decisions, or constitute unauthorized practice in restricted fields.

ai_subtech.description: Intellectual Property Compromise: Intellectual Property Infringement
ai_subtech.definition:
Prompts, content, or outputs, whether expressed as text, audio, visual material, actions, operations, or other modes from LLMs (as well as the use of agentic AI systems, their components, or elements) that enable, promote, or facilitate unauthorized use, reproduction, or distribution of copyrighted or trademarked material.
This may include generating or automating instructions for piracy, producing infringing content such as unlicensed songs, films, or books, or misusing branded material.
Agentic systems and their components may be exploited to scale piracy, automate infringement, or disguise unlawful distribution, amplifying risks for creators, businesses, and intellectual property rights.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0048.004: ML Intellectual Property Theft

ai_subtech.definition:
Prompts, content, or outputs, whether expressed as text, audio, visual material, actions, operations, or other modes from LLMs (as well as the use of agentic AI systems, their components, or elements) that are not generally known or available to the public and are considered sensitive or private.
This may include generating or automating instructions for collecting, producing, harvesting or getting different sensitive information or data not intended for general exposure or use.
Agentic systems and their components may be exploited to scale expose, collect, or produce sensitive data.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0048.004: ML Intellectual Property Theft

ai_subtech.definition:
Prompts, content, or outputs, whether expressed as text, audio, visual material, actions, operations, or other modes from LLMs (as well as the use of agentic AI systems, their components, or elements) that expose, generate, or misuse personally identifiable information (PII), protected health information (PHI), or payment card industry (PCI) information.
For PII, this may include names, addresses, phone numbers, identification numbers, or other sensitive personal details that can be used to identify, track, or exploit individuals.
For PHI, this may include any individually identifiable health data, such as medical records, diagnoses, billing information, and other personal identifiers.
For PCI, this may include cardholder data (e.g., cardholder names, expiration dates, service code) and sensitive authentication data (e.g., full track data, card verification codes, and personal identification numbers (PINs)).
Agentic systems may also be abused to collect, reconstruct, or target specific individuals’ data, amplifying risks of privacy violations, identity theft, and harassment.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0057: LLM Data Leakage
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0025: Exfiltration via Cyber Means
NIST AML: NISTAML.036: Leaking Information from User Interactions

OB-016.definition: Actors conduct unauthorized passive monitoring, interception, or extraction of data from model/agentic system communications, including agent-to-agent messages, agent-to-user interactions, API calls, or other internal communications without directly manipulating the data flow.

ai_tech.definition: Actors conduct unauthorized passive monitoring, interception, or extraction of data from model/agentic system communications, including agent-to-agent messages, agent-to-user interactions, API calls, or other internal communications without directly manipulating the data flow.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0037: Data From Local System
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0069: LLM System Information Discovery
MITRE ATT&CK: T1213: Data from Information Repositories
NIST AML: NISTAML.036: Leaking Information from User Interactions

ai_subtech.definition: Storing or recording user-model interactions in ways that include personally identifiable information (PII), private data, or sensitive content, without adequate consent, anonymization, security, or retention limits (which could eventually be leaked or misused).
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0037: Data From Local System
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0069: LLM System Information Discovery
MITRE ATT&CK: T1213: Data from Information Repositories
NIST AML: NISTAML.036: Leaking Information from User Interactions

OB-017.definition: Actors use of highly-realistic but deceptive signals or manipulating sensor data to deliberately mislead an AI system and its decision-making processes through falsified sensory inputs.

ai_tech.definition: Actors use of highly-realistic but deceptive signals or manipulating sensor data to deliberately mislead an AI system and its decision-making processes through falsified sensory inputs.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0043: Craft Adversarial Data
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0015: Evade AI Model

ai_subtech.definition: Data points or signals that prompt a model to undertake specific actions beyond reasoning. These signals can be malicious or benign, allowing agents to execute operations in physical or digital environments.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0043: Craft Adversarial Data
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0015: Evade AI Model

OB-018.definition: Actors leverage models/agents specifically for generating, executing, scaling, or automating fraud, scams, deception, and other illegal activities.

ai_tech.definition: Actors leverage models/agents specifically for generating, executing, scaling, or automating fraud, scams, deception, and other illegal activities.
mappings: OWASP: LLM04:2025: Data and Model Poisoning
MITRE ATT&CK: T1588.007: Obtain AI Capabilities

ai_subtech.definition: Automating generation of large volumes of unsolicited or fraudulent content (phishing, fake offers, spam emails/messages, impersonation or manipulation of trust) to deceive people or solicit funds, credentials, personal and confidential information, or other valuables.
mappings: OWASP: LLM04:2025: Data and Model Poisoning
MITRE ATT&CK: T1588.007: Obtain AI Capabilities

ai_tech.definition: Actors leverage AI models to generate, execute, and scale harmful activities like cyberattacks, data theft, and disinformation campaigns. among other deceptive purposes.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0040: AI Model Inference API Access
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0049: Exploit Public Facing Application

ai_subtech.description: Abuse of APIs for Mass Automation
ai_subtech.definition: Leveraging APIs in bulk for malicious purpose at scale, including flooding attacks, automation of worst-case prompts, or executing workflows that negatively impact many users or systems.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0040: AI Model Inference API Access
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0049: Exploit Public Facing Application

ai_subtech.definition: Establishing purpose-built servers, infrastructure, or services specifically designed to support, scale, or automate AI-powered attacks, malicious workflows, or harmful operations.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0002: Acquire Infrastructure / Develop Capabilities
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0005: Create Proxy AI Model
NIST AML: NISTAML.05: Supply Chain Attacks

OB-019.definition: Actors exploit contradictions, mismatches, or inconsistencies between different data modalities (i.e., text, image, audio, video) to confuse AI systems, bypass security controls, evade detection, or manipulate decision-making by presenting conflicting information across modalities that the system cannot properly reconcile.

ai_tech.definition: Actors exploit contradictions, mismatches, or inconsistencies between different data modalities (i.e., text, image, audio, video) to confuse AI systems, bypass security controls, evade detection, or manipulate decision-making by presenting conflicting information across modalities that the system cannot properly reconcile.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0043: Craft Adversarial Data
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0015: Evade AI Model

ai_subtech.definition: Exploiting a model's inability to consistently handle conflicting instructions by embedding deceptive or contradictory commands within user input across or within modalities, causing behavior drift toward malicious objectives.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0043: Craft Adversarial Data

ai_subtech.definition: Manipulating one modality (e.g., corrupting audio transcript, poisoning image metadata) to bias arbitration mechanisms toward favoring the manipulated channel over others.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0015: Evade AI Model

ai_tech.definition: Actors exploit weaknesses in fusion algorithms, arbitration logic, and decision-making processes to manipulate final system behavior. Combining payload components delivered through different inputs, outputs, or sources (including data sources, multiple models, or agents) and using the system’s fusion or arbitration mechanisms to assemble them to create an injection or attack payload.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0019: Publish Poisoned Dataset
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0058: Publish Poisoned Models
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0018: Manipulate AI Model
OWASP: LLM04:2025: Data and Model Poisoning

ai_subtech.definition: Injecting adversarial data into training or other data sources across any modality to corrupt joint embeddings or fusion layers and establish a hidden payload that can later be activated. One part of the payload is embedded during data poisoning, while the other part is delivered at runtime. These components are combined to produces an attack or injection payload.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0019: Publish Poisoned Dataset
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0058: Publish Poisoned Models
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0018: Manipulate AI Model
OWASP: LLM04:2025: Data and Model Poisoning

ai_subtech.definition: Crafting partial or complementary payload components across any modality, source, or agent output that, when fused by the system, contribute to producing an attack. Both parts of the payload are delivered at runtime, and when the system combines these components through its fusion or arbitration mechanisms, they form an attack or injection payload.
mappings: MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0015: Evade AI Model
